algorithms.title: The Disaster Cycle — Health Professional's Role by Phase
algorithms.description: The four continuous phases of emergency management and what health professionals contribute in each.
steps: Mitigation: support measures that reduce future impact — safe facilities, building codes, immunization, community risk reduction.
Preparedness: write and exercise plans, train (BDLS/ADLS), stockpile, prepare yourself and your family.
Response: operate inside incident command using the DISASTER Paradigm — triage, treat, and protect yourself and the scene.
Recovery: restore services, support community and responder mental health, capture lessons learned.
Feed lessons learned back into mitigation and planning — the cycle never ends.

algorithms.title: PRE-DISASTER Readiness Sequence
algorithms.description: Pre-event readiness per the PRE-DISASTER Paradigm, from the individual to the community level.
steps: Plan: personal/family plan, facility plan, community plan — with clear roles.
Practice: drill and exercise the plans; update contacts and supplies after every exercise.
Build resilience: hardened infrastructure, redundant systems, healthy and prepared people.
Educate and train: competency-based courses so all responders share one lexicon.
Re-assess hazards and repeat — readiness decays without maintenance.
